Title: Creation of male-sterile lines that can be restored to fertility by exogenous methyl jasmonate for the establishment of a two-line system for the hybrid production of rice (Oryza sativa L.)

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family; OPR (12-oxophytodienoate reductase)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OPDA reduction step in JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: JA precursor downstream of OPDA reduction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxophytodienoic acid in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway, contributing to jasmonic acid production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor pool required for jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential step for JA biosynthesis in the context of anther dehiscence, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsOPR7 function causes jasmonate-defective male sterility with indehiscent anthers, reduced pollen viability, and near-zero seed set without methyl jasmonate; exogenous MeJA partially restores fertility and seed setting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Jasmonic acid / methyl jasmonate-responsive jasmonate pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for anther dehiscence, pollen viability, fertility restoration, and practical maintenance of male-sterile lines for hybrid seed production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in anthers relative to osopr7 mutants; WT anthers showed much higher OsOPR7 transcript levels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Anther-associated expression; measured in anthers and strongly reduced in T1 osopr7 family plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout editing of exon 2 and exon 4; mutant genotyping by sequencing; RT-qPCR; pollen KI-I2 staining; floral/anther phenotype analysis; exogenous methyl jasmonate rescue; seed-setting and agronomic trait measurements
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter (constitutive, driving mpCas9); rU6 promoter (constitutive, driving sgRNA)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous methyl jasmonate spraying (500 µM), three times daily for 15 days before anthesis; normal paddy field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Untreated mutants are completely male sterile with 0% seed set; MeJA restoration is partial (~17.9%–20.4% seed set in T2 examples), requiring labor or drone spraying; improper MeJA use may risk floral side effects based on prior studies in other species
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsOPR7 is the rice ortholog of Arabidopsis OPR3. Editing exon 2 generated more allelic variants than editing exon 4, suggesting higher mutagenesis efficiency at exon 2. Male sterility was described as stable and not dependent on light or temperature.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsOPR7 was identified as the key experimentally manipulated gene for generating a jasmonate-defective male-sterile rice line. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of OsOPR7 caused indehiscent anthers, reduced pollen viability, and complete or near-complete male sterility. 3. Exogenous methyl jasmonate restored anther dehiscence and partial fertility, enabling maintenance of the sterile line for a proposed two-line hybrid rice production system.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A practical target for engineering phytohormone-genic male sterility in rice; osopr7 lines can serve as female parents in a two-line hybrid system and be maintained by MeJA spraying. Optimization of spray timing, frequency, and concentration is needed to improve seed set.
